appliedUpdates.description: Added 'start: PORT=3100 pnpm start' command for production server, matching the PORT convention used by the dev command

Documented the dual-panel responsive pattern (mobile bottom sheet + desktop side panel, both in DOM with CSS toggle) in architecture.md to prevent future workers from being surprised by duplicate DOM elements in tests

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add TDD exemption guidance to fullstack-worker, ui-worker, and map-worker skills: the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle should be skipped for pure data corrections, color/value changes, CSS positioning changes, responsive CSS work, and verification-only features. These are not new functionality and TDD adds no value.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 5 of 6 features had workers skip or deviate from the TDD step. fix-place-coordinates (data fix), change-clubs-color (value swap), move-search-filter-top-right (CSS positioning), responsive-mobile-polish (responsive CSS), and build-deploy-integration (verification only) all correctly skipped TDD. Workers noted this in skillFeedback across fullstack-worker, ui-worker, and map-worker skills.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note to map-worker and ui-worker skills that manual browser verification (agent-browser) is optional for code-level fixes fully covered by unit tests. Visual verification adds overhead with minimal value when changes are to refs, callback dependencies, keyboard handlers, or other non-visual behaviors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-place-coordinates and misc-polish-fixes workers both skipped browser verification. misc-polish-fixes had 10 comprehensive unit tests covering all three fixes (beforeId, zoomRef, keyboard nav). fix-place-coordinates was a pure data coordinate update with no visual verification value given WebGL limitations in headless Chrome.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a verification-only procedure path to the fullstack-worker skill for features that require no code changes (integration testing, build verification, deploy readiness checks). The current skill assumes implementation work with RED/GREEN cycles.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: build-deploy-integration worker had to adapt the skill procedure since no code changes were needed. Worker suggested this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker didn't explicitly run pnpm lint for change-clubs-color feature
rejectedObservations.reason: Individual oversight for a trivial 3-line change, not a systemic issue. Lint was run by the scrutiny validator and passed. Not worth a skill change.

rejectedObservations.observation: next lint deprecation warning about Next.js 16 migration
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing issue visible to anyone running lint. Not actionable until Next.js 16 upgrade is planned. Already documented in lint output.

rejectedObservations.observation: beforeId 'water_name_point_label' hardcoded as string literal without shared constant from map-style.ts
rejectedObservations.reason: Valid code improvement but too granular for a convention or library entry. This is a specific refactoring suggestion, not a pattern to document.
